element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core melee combat against initial enemy types
Ranged combat option usable freely on isometric field (not in combat mode)
Goblin king spare triggers ally status; navigating faction warfare
Resource management for potion and item purchases

element_introduction_order.element: Magical items and objects (some deadly if misused)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Risk assessment; learning which items are lethal traps
Alternative vertical traversal and puzzle solving
Advanced enemy variety; handling unseen threats

element_introduction_order.element: Flesh-eating slimes, giant worms, colossal spider

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Lore consumption; ancient dragon/Erinoch civilization backstory
Culmination of all learned mechanics and lore

typical_relief_triggers: Resting at straw beds (safe zones with narrative dreams)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Linear progression with escalating threat variety; combat-frequent with intermittent safe exploration; narrative tension builds through dream exposition revealing betrayal

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Chests and corpses to loot; merchant presence indicates optional transactions available

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic melee and fireball in controlled encounters; goblins/trolls provide moderate opposition
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Currency-based resource management emerges; dangerous item acquisition introduces risk; magical traversal abilities expand mobility; enemy variety increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced enemy types (invisible, flying, boss-sized creatures); water-based puzzles; narrative twist raises emotional stakes; final boss synthesizes all mechanics
notable_spikes: First invisible shade encounters (unseen threat paradigm shift)
Giant worm and colossal spider (size/health escalation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Straw beds serve as narrative breathing points with dream sequences; provides healing/rest without combat pressure; establishes sanctuary rhythm
NPC merchant in specific levels offers resource exchange (gold for potions/items); gates progression on item availability; disguise twist revealed late
8 labyrinth levels form vertical descent; no world map; progression is gated; creates escalating depth metaphor with danger increasing downward
Goblins/trolls at war; player spares goblin king to gain alliance; introduces consequence-based NPC interactions; affects combat resource availability
Flying carpet, levitation, barrel paddle obtained mid-late game; each unlocks new traversal possibility; expands spatial expressiveness without player choice
